tutorials.title: Training Adapters Tutorial
tutorials.description: Learn how to train adapters step by step

steps.title: Welcome to Training
steps.content: This tutorial will guide you through training an adapter. Training creates specialized AI models for your domain.

steps.title: Choose a Template
steps.content: Start by selecting a training template. Templates provide pre-configured settings for common use cases.

steps.title: Configure Parameters
steps.content: Adjust training parameters like learning rate, batch size, and epochs to match your requirements.

steps.title: Start Training
steps.content: Click the Start Training button to launch your training job. Monitor progress in real-time.

tutorials.title: Managing Adapters
tutorials.description: Learn how to deploy and manage adapters

steps.title: Adapter Management
steps.content: Adapters are specialized AI models trained for specific domains. Learn how to deploy and manage them.

steps.title: Deploy an Adapter
steps.content: Click Deploy to make a trained adapter available for inference. Ensure it has passed all tests first.

steps.title: Monitor Health
steps.content: Check adapter health metrics regularly. Look for latency, error rates, and resource usage.

tutorials.title: Policy Management
tutorials.description: Learn how to manage policies and ensure compliance

steps.title: Policy Management
steps.content: Policies enforce security and compliance rules. This tutorial shows you how to review and manage them.

steps.title: Review Policy Packs
steps.content: All 20 policy packs should be reviewed regularly. Click on a policy to view its details.

steps.title: Sign Policies
steps.content: After reviewing, sign policies to indicate compliance. Unsigned policies may block operations.

tutorials.title: Dashboard Overview
tutorials.description: Learn how to navigate and use the dashboard

steps.title: Welcome to the Dashboard
steps.content: The dashboard provides a system overview with health metrics, adapter counts, and performance indicators.

steps.title: System Health
steps.content: Monitor system health metrics here. Green indicates healthy, yellow means attention needed, red requires immediate action.

steps.title: Recent Activity
steps.content: View recent system events and activities. This helps you stay informed about what's happening.
